title: Par Number PASS: Fib_hits observations for par-classified primes
_fixture_purpose: Documents the relationship between par class and Fibonacci zero-count within Pisano periods. The pattern is nuanced: for primes p, Fib_hits depends on Legendre symbol (5/p). This fixture records observed values without enforcing a universal rule. The QA connection: par class predicts the quadratic character of 5 mod p, which governs Pisano period structure.
source_grounding: Primary source: Wall, D.D. (1960), Fibonacci Series Modulo m, American Mathematical Monthly 67(6):525-532, DOI:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 -- rank of apparition and Pisano period theory (also cited by cert [291]).

fib_witnesses.note: 5-par prime; pi(29)=14; hits=1. Corrected 2026-07-06: (5/29)=+1 (5 IS a quadratic residue mod 29, since 11^2=121=5 mod 29; independently verified via Euler's criterion 5^14 mod 29=1 and by exhaustive QR enumeration), not -1 as previously stated. A bare Legendre(5,p) value does not by itself determine hits count: p=41 has Legendre(5,41)=+1 but 2 hits, not 1 -- the true determinant is the rank of apparition alpha(p) (smallest k with p|F_k) relative to pi(p), a more refined invariant this fixture does not attempt to characterize.

validation_checks.description: 4 Fib_hits values verified against Pisano computation
